title: Falcosidekick Output Forwarding
description:
## Falcosidekick Output Forwarding

Falcosidekick is a companion tool that extends Falco's alert delivery capabilities.

How does Falcosidekick receive alerts from the Falco daemon?

Choose the correct option:

options.text: By parsing the syslog daemon /var/log/syslog on the host system.
By listening on an HTTP endpoint where the Falco daemon posts alerts in JSON format.
By mounting the exact same SQLite database that Falco writes to.
By intercepting the standard output of the Falco daemon pods.

explanation: Falco is configured to output alerts over HTTP by enabling the `http` output option and pointing it to Falcosidekick's server endpoint (usually `http://falco-falcosidekick:2801/`). Falcosidekick listens on this port, parses the JSON payloads, and forwards them to Slack, Elasticsearch, or other outputs.

hints.title: Falcosidekick Architecture Docs
hints.body: Falcosidekick acts as a fan-out forwarder. Falco communicates with it via standard HTTP webhook POSTs.
